---
title: "hollys"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-26"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
orig <- read_csv("hollysdata_nil.csv")
orig %>% filter(repetition==1) %>% group_by(subject, clinicalStatus) %>% summarise(N = n(), RT=mean(RT)) -> orig_avg
recovered <- read_csv("holly_model_comparison_summary.csv")
names(recovered)[1] <- "subject"
data <- orig_avg %>% left_join(recovered, by = c("subject"))
```

```{r, fig.width=5, fig.height=5}

data %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = c("d", "phi", "c", "w1"), names_to = "parameter", values_to = "value") -> data_long
data_long %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=clinicalStatus, y=value, col=clinicalStatus)) +
  scale_color_aaas() +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~parameter, scales="free_y") +
  geom_boxplot(alpha=0.1) +
  geom_signif(
    comparisons = list(c("HC", "MCI")),
    col="black",
    map_signif_level = TRUE, textsize = 3
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position ="bottom",
        
    strip.text.x = element_text(margin = margin(b = 10, unit = "pt")), # Increase bottom margin
    strip.text.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10, unit = "pt"))  # Increase right margin (if faceting by rows)
  )
  labs(title="Model Parameters by Clinical Status",
       x="Clinical Status",
       y="Parameter Value")
```

```{r, fig.width=4, fig.height=4}
ggplot(data, aes(x=p, y=phi, col=clinicalStatus)) +
  scale_color_aaas() +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position ="bottom") +
  labs(title="Parameter Correlations",
       x="FEAR Decay (d)",
       y="ACT-R phi (phi)")
```

```{r}
variables <- c("c", "phi", "t0_actr", "F_actr", "d", "w1", "t0_fear", "F_fear")
library(corrplot)
library(Hmisc)
R <-  data[variables] %>% as.matrix() %>% rcorr(type="pearson") 

corrplot(R$r, method = "circle",  diag = F, 
         type = "full", tl.col = "black", tl.srt = 45, number.cex = 0.5,
         p.mat = R$P, sig.level = 0.05, insig = "pch", pch=".", tl.cex=1,
         col=colorRampPalette(c("royalblue4", "ivory1", "firebrick4"))(200),
         cl.align.text = "l", cl.cex=1,
         addgrid.col="white",
         title = "Correlation Between Parameters",
         mar=c(0,0,1,0))


```
    

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
